resources.string: Equation: A=B for some variable values. Unknowns x,y,z; parameters a,b,c.
Root makes equality true. Equivalent equations share roots. Transform carefully to avoid false roots.
Linear eq: ax=b (a≠0). First-degree equation in one unknown.
If a≠0 → x=b/a. If a=0 → none or ∞ roots. ax=x+1 → one root if a≠1.
Quadratic: ax²+bx+c=0, a≠0. D=b²−4ac decides roots. Formula: x=(-b±√D)/2a. Viète: x₁+x₂=-b/a, x₁x₂=c/a.
Viète links roots and coefficients. D>0→2 roots, D=0→1, D<0→none.
Factor form: a(x−x₁)(x−x₂), from Viète’s theorem.
Higher-degree eq: a₀xⁿ+...+aₙ=0. Use factoring or substitution. Biquadratic: ax⁴+bx²+c=0 → y=x².
Factoring lowers degree. Substitution simplifies radicals. Check for extraneous roots.
Equations with several unknowns (e.g., x+2y=3). Solutions are ordered pairs/triples.
Systems solved simultaneously; may have one, none, or ∞ solutions.
System: a₁x+b₁y=c₁; a₂x+b₂y=c₂. Solve by substitution or addition.
Cases: unique, none, or ∞. Lines intersect, parallel, or coincide.
Maintain equivalence. Analyze parameter effects and domains. Substitution helps simplify.
Handle linear, quadratic, irrational, and system equations with parameters carefully.
Word Problems: define unknowns, express conditions, form equations, choose solving method.
Examples: number, motion, work, flow, or team problems; form and solve systems.
Inequalities: >, <, ≥, ≤. Domain = valid variable values. Solution makes statement true.
Use transitivity, addition, multiplication rules. Equivalent inequalities share solutions.
Prove inequalities via direct/equivalent transformations. Use AM-GM, triangle, modulus properties.
Reduce to known forms like (a−b)²≥0. Apply mean and modulus inequalities.
Linear: ax+b>0 → ray. Quadratic: ax²+bx+c>0 → depends on D and sign of a.
Systems of inequalities → intersection of solution sets. |x|≤a ⇔ -a≤x≤a; |x|≥a ⇔ x≥a or x≤-a.
Method of Intervals: find roots of P,Q; mark on axis; check signs; select valid intervals.
